item.title: French weather service alerts police to tampering after suspicious Polymarket bets
item.description: Users of weather forum say Paris temperature data may have been manipulated for wagers on prediction market platform

item.title: DeepSeek targets $20bn valuation to stop poaching of staff
item.description: Chinese AI start-up is raising funds for first time to keep researchers after several defections to rivals

item.title: Europe’s resistance to Chinese investment has stalled
item.description: The European Commission’s repeated attempts to run a coherent green industrial strategy are showing few results

item.title: Deutsche Telekom boss braces for battle to land US mega-merger
item.description: Tim Höttges must win support from the German government to secure landmark $267bn deal for T-Mobile US

item.title: PwC fined $166mn by Hong Kong regulators over Evergrande audit
item.description: Big Four firm also faces six-month ban related to its work on failed property developer’s books

item.title: What does AI really mean for your work? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with Sarah O’Connor, John Burn Murdoch and Madhumita Murgia today at 1pm BST

item.title: US to speed up payments for new medical devices
item.description: Regulators hand industry a lobbying victory after fierce effort to get faster coverage for their products

item.title: Man Group hit by single $6.1bn redemption
item.description: Withdrawal from one investor threatens to slow efforts to expand beyond hedge fund division

item.title: L’Oréal hails ‘lipstick effect’ as war drives sales of small beauty comforts
item.description: Cosmetics group’s chief also credits recovery in Chinese market as revenues in Europe and north Asia grow

item.title: Foxtons suffers 35% slump in house sale fees
item.description: Iran war has hit buyer sentiment and affected mortgage rates and availability, says chief executive

item.title: Nestlé sales held back by infant formula recalls
item.description: Swiss group beats expectations as coffee growth offsets impact of baby product saga

item.title: Sainsbury’s and WHSmith warn war will hit customers and business
item.description: Retailers flag weak outlook as consumers rein in spending

item.title: Beijing’s auto bash and Coupang under scrutiny
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: UK public sector borrowing hit £12.6bn in March as Iran war strains finances
item.description: Higher energy prices stoked by conflict have resulted in higher borrowing costs for government

item.title: A vibe check from commodities pros
item.description: Plus UK inflation

item.title: EU leaders to grapple with energy crisis as Brussels pleads for calm response
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Paring back permitting

item.title: FirstFT: UK crime agency reviewed Mandelson allegations in 2024
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US Navy secretary fired and EU Arctic drilling rethink

item.title: Japan moves to block foreign takeover deal on national security grounds
item.description: Government asks South Korean private equity group MBK Partners to drop attempt to buy Makino Milling

item.title: Escape from Hormuz: the oil tankers running the Iranian gauntlet
item.description: How trading houses Vitol, Trafigura and Mercuria have managed to get some of their oil tankers out of the Gulf

item.title: PE firm L Catterton launches new fund staked by top athletes
item.description: LVMH-backed group will seek to harness influence of sports personalities to enhance portfolio companies

item.title: Private equity courts OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: The start-ups are in talks with private equity firms over joint ventures intended to help deploy AI in businesses

item.title: Nelson Peltz’s son builds first public activist stake in Intertek
item.description: Matt Peltz’s new firm Lost Coast Collective has invested in the UK testing company

item.title: Quant pioneer Martin Lueck warns against handing over trading to AI
item.description: Caution by co-founder of Aspect hedge fund follows billionaire Cliff Asness’s decision to ‘surrender’ to the machines

item.title: House of Fidelity — the dynasty at the heart of the investment powerhouse
item.description: A journalist lifts the lid on how the Johnson family battled to keep control of the asset manager in a history packed with luminaries of finance

item.title: UK could save £2.5bn by helping banks to buy gilts, says Barclays
item.description: Idea long pursued by banking sector lobbyists faces opposition from regulators

item.title: Top Republican pushes party to shun $300mn AI lobby
item.description: Senator Josh Hawley warns of ‘political cost’ if Washington fails to rein in Big Tech and artificial intelligence

item.title: High earners race ahead on AI as workplace divide widens
item.description: FT-Focaldata poll offers early evidence on how the technology is reshaping work and reinforcing gender and pay gaps

item.title: Will energy security fears change the global energy market? With Daniel Yergin
item.description: What the Iran conflict means for oil stockpiles, gas and renewables

item.title: The AI digital divide
item.description: An FT survey shows the highest-earning workers are adopting the technology in their jobs far faster than others

item.title: SpaceX and Tesla are on an inevitable collision course
item.description: To the extent shareholders just want exposure to Elon Musk, a single security seems like the sensible end state
